_meta.description: Historical timeline data for Rocky Mount - key dates and events in site history

_aiRules.description: Rules for AI assistants editing this file
beforeEditing: ALWAYS verify changes with user before committing
ALWAYS verify historical claims against documented sources before adding
prohibited: Do NOT make changes without user awareness
Do NOT add historical events without scholarly sources
Do NOT modify established dates without documented evidence
Do NOT add speculative or unverified historical information
fileSpecific: Historical accuracy is paramount - all dates and facts must be verified
Cite sources in commit messages when adding new historical events
Consult with site historians for pre-1800 additions
Keep heroTimeline to exactly 3 items - discuss with team before adding
Only 2-3 events should have highlight: true
Use four-digit year as string format: '1790' not 1790

sourceCitation.requirement: All new historical claims must reference a documented source

_aiRules.whenUnsure: Ask the user for clarification - historical accuracy is critical

whenToEdit: Adding a new historical event with documented sources
Correcting historical dates or facts based on new research
Expanding the extended timeline with more detail
whenNotToEdit: Changing established dates without scholarly sources
staffInstructions.howToEdit: On GitHub: Navigate to data/timeline.json > Click pencil icon > Make changes > Click 'Commit changes'
historicalStandards: All dates must be historically verified
Descriptions should be factual, not promotional
Note sources in commit message when adding new events
Consult with site historians for pre-1800 additions

heroTimeline.description: The main 3-point timeline shown on homepage - most important moments only
heroTimeline.purpose: Quick visual story of Rocky Mount's significance

fields.title: One-word or very short title (Settlement, Revolution, Capital)
fields.highlight: Set to true for the most important moment (usually 1790)

extendedTimeline.description: Comprehensive timeline with more detail for education/discovery pages

fields.title: Short event title (2-5 words)
fields.category: Event type for filtering and styling
fields.highlight: Set to true for especially significant events

contrastStatements.description: Rhetorical device used in storytelling sections

fields.strike: true if this statement should appear crossed out
fields.finale: true for the final, emphasized statement

contrastStatements.note: Do not edit unless redesigning the narrative structure

keyDates.description: Quick-reference lookup for important dates used programmatically
keyDates.purpose: Allows code to reference dates without hardcoding
keyDates.note: Staff rarely need to edit - these are structural

rules.dateFormat: Always use four-digit year as a string: '1790' not 1790 or '90
rules.duplicateYears: Multiple events in same year are allowed - add them consecutively
rules.highlightSparingly: Only 2-3 events should have highlight: true

newHeroTimelineEvent.description: For major moments only - heroTimeline should stay at 3 items
newHeroTimelineEvent.note: Rarely add to heroTimeline - discuss with team first

newExtendedTimelineEvent.description: Copy this to add a new event to the detailed timeline

example.title: Event Title Here
example.description: One sentence describing what happened and why it matters

newHighlightedEvent.description: For especially significant events that should stand out

categories.diplomacy: Treaties and relations with Native nations

examples: Governor Blount made it the seat of government.
William Cobb establishes homestead on Sinking Creek
Governor William Blount establishes capital at Rocky Mount
Tennessee becomes 16th state of the Union

tips: Use active voice (Blount establishes... not The capital was established...)
Connect local events to larger historical context
Keep language accessible to general audiences

keyFigures.williamCobb: Original settler who built the cabin, patriarch of the Cobb family
keyFigures.williamBlount: First and only governor of the Southwest Territory (1790-1796)
keyFigures.overrmountainMen: Frontier militia who fought at Kings Mountain

keyEvents.southwestTerritory: Created by Congress in 1790 from land ceded by North Carolina
keyEvents.treatyOfHolston: 1791 treaty negotiated at White's Fort (Knoxville), following preliminary talks at Rocky Mount (Dec 1790)
keyEvents.kingsmountain: 1780 battle where Overmountain Men defeated British forces

heroTimeline.title: Capital
heroTimeline.text: Governor Blount made it the seat of government for the Southwest Territory.

extendedTimeline.title: Cobb Family Settles
extendedTimeline.description: William Cobb establishes homestead on Sinking Creek

extendedTimeline.title: Overmountain Men Muster
extendedTimeline.description: Frontier militia gathers for Kings Mountain campaign

extendedTimeline.title: Southwest Territory Created
extendedTimeline.description: Congress establishes territory from North Carolina cession

extendedTimeline.title: Blount Arrives
extendedTimeline.description: Governor William Blount establishes capital at Rocky Mount

extendedTimeline.title: Treaty of Holston
extendedTimeline.description: Treaty signed at White's Fort (Knoxville), following preliminary talks at Rocky Mount

extendedTimeline.title: Tennessee Statehood
extendedTimeline.description: Tennessee becomes 16th state of the Union

extendedTimeline.title: State Historic Site
extendedTimeline.description: Rocky Mount designated Tennessee State Historic Site

contrastStatements.text: This is not where they gathered.
This is not where they farmed.
